todays_candidates:
Web search access was denied, so I'm working from the provided market context and my knowledge through August 2025. I cannot verify current prices, live 50-day MA levels, or exact May 2026 earnings dates — so treat this as a **research shortlist requiring your own pre-market verification** before any entry.

---

Given the market context (risk-on, AI/semis leading, oil up ~1.1%, defensives lagging), here are the candidates most aligned with the strategy:

---

**SYMBOL:** AVGO (Broadcom)
**THESIS:** AI networking chip demand is accelerating as hyperscalers expand custom silicon programs, and AVGO has been a consistent beneficiary with strong recurring revenue from VMware integration.
**ENTRY_AROUND:** Verify vs. current price — target entry near or just above the 50-day MA on any intraday pullback
**STOP_LOSS:** 7% below entry
**TARGET:** +15–20% above entry
**CHECK BEFORE ENTRY:** Confirm no earnings within 3 trading days (Broadcom reports quarterly ~late June and early September — likely clear in late May, but verify)

---

**SYMBOL:** MRVL (Marvell Technology)
**THESIS:** Custom AI ASIC demand from cloud hyperscalers and 5G infrastructure is a strong catalyst, and analyst upgrades have been frequent in 2025; price momentum has tracked the broader semis rally.
**ENTRY_AROUND:** Verify vs. 50-day MA — mid-$70s to $80s range was relevant in late 2024/early 2025; confirm current level
**STOP_LOSS:** 7% below entry
**TARGET:** +15–20% above entry
**CHECK BEFORE ENTRY:** MRVL typically reports in late May/early June — **earnings date must be confirmed clear of the 3-day window before entry**

---

**SYMBOL:** AMD (Advanced Micro Devices)
**THESIS:** MI300X GPU ramp for AI inference workloads provides a credible second-source story to NVDA; strong data center revenue growth with less crowded positioning.
**ENTRY_AROUND:** Verify current level vs. 50-day MA
**STOP_LOSS:** 7% below entry
**TARGET:** +15–20% above entry
**CHECK BEFORE ENTRY:** AMD Q1 2026 earnings likely already reported (typically late April); confirm clear

---

**SYMBOL:** OXY (Occidental Petroleum)
**THESIS:** Leveraged to WTI upside — OXY has higher operating leverage vs. majors, and with oil up ~1.1% today on supply concerns, energy is a rare non-tech sector showing strength aligned with the risk-on tape.
**ENTRY_AROUND:** Verify vs. 50-day MA — OXY trades loosely with WTI; near-term support matters
**STOP_LOSS:** 7% below entry
**TARGET:** +15–20% above entry
**CHECK BEFORE ENTRY:** No major OXY earnings expected in late May (Q1 typically reported in late April)

---

**SYMBOL:** MSFT (Microsoft)
**THESIS:** Azure AI growth narrative is intact, Copilot monetization is expanding, and MSFT is a lower-beta way to ride the AI wave if you want reduced single-stock vol vs. semis.
**ENTRY_AROUND:** Verify vs. 50-day MA — likely well above given recent AI momentum
**STOP_LOSS:** 7% below entry
**TARGET:** +15–20% above entry
**CHECK BEFORE ENTRY:** MSFT Q3 FY2026 earnings likely already out (typically late April); confirm clear; also check RSI — if extended above 70, skip

---

## Pre-Market Checklist Before Entry

For **each** candidate above, confirm:
- [ ] Price is above the 50-day MA right now
- [ ] RSI(14) is below 70
- [ ] No earnings announcement in the next 3 trading days (May 21–23)
- [ ] No negative pre-market news specific to the stock
- [ ] S&P 500 is above its 200-day MA (market regime check)
- [ ] Volume is tracking above average in the first 30 min after open

**Skip any candidate that fails even one check.** With no current positions, discipline on entry quality matters more than filling a full book today.
